quick_skim.title: Shield-Loco: Shielding Locomotion Policies with Predictive Safety Filtering
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) policies enable dynamic legged locomotion but lack mechanisms to avoid violations of safety constraints that are absent during training. Large-scale offline safe learning is impractical for covering all edge cases. Existing safety frameworks either rely on reduced-order models that cannot reason about whole-body behaviors or require conservative recovery controllers that degrade task performance. We propose a predictive safety filter that post-hoc filters the nominal contact locations fed to the RL policy. When a collision is predicted, a sampling-based optimizer asynchronously searches for safer contact sequences using a full-physics model, while a learned value function bootstraps long-horizon returns. Our three algorithmic components (geometric projection of sampled contacts, momentum-augmented updates, and replica-exchange) make the optimization tractable in a discontinuous contact landscape. We validate the filter on a quadruped robot in dense, cluttered environments, both in simulation and in the real world, showing substantial reductions in safety violations with minimal deviation from the nominal input.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: predictive safety filtering for RL locomotion policies, similar to CBF
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 为RL运动策略提供预测性安全过滤，类似CBF思想
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 为RL运动策略提供预测性安全过滤，类似CBF思想
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 为RL运动策略提供预测性安全过滤，类似CBF思想
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Shield-Loco uses a predictive safety filter (sampling-based optimizer with full physics) to post-hoc guard RL locomotion policies from constraint violations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对强化学习运动策略缺乏安全保证的问题，提出Shield-Loco预测安全过滤器。当预测到碰撞时，异步采样优化器基于全物理模型搜索安全接触序列，同时学习值函数引导长期回报。三种算法在仿真和现实平台上验证了有效性，保持了任务性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对强化学习运动策略缺乏安全保证的问题，提出Shield-Loco预测安全过滤器。当预测到碰撞时，异步采样优化器基于全物理模型搜索安全接触序列，同时学习值函数引导长期回报。三种算法在仿真和现实平台上验证了有效性，保持了任务性能。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Safe reinforcement learning integrated with control barrier functions for autonomous systems

quick_skim.title: MuJoCo-Drones-Gym: A GPU-Accelerated Multi-Drone Simulator for Control and Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Robotic simulators are a cornerstone of modern research in aerial robotics, serving both as a vehicle for the development of new control algorithms and as the data source for training reinforcement learning (RL) policies. Yet, existing quadcopter learning environments often face a trade-off between physical fidelity, multi-agent support, and the throughput required by modern deep RL pipelines. In this paper, we present MuJoCo-Drones-Gym, an open-source Gymnasium-compatible multi-drone environment built on top of the MuJoCo physics engine. MuJoCo-Drones-Gym supports an arbitrary number of Bitcraze Crazyflie 2.x nano-quadcopters and exposes a modular API for selecting (i)~the physics model (rigid-body MuJoCo, explicit Python dynamics, or any subset of ground effect, blade drag, and inter-drone downwash), (ii)~the action interface (per-motor RPMs, collective normalized thrust, velocity setpoints, or PID waypoint commands), and (iii)~the observation space (kinematic state vectors, RGB / depth / segmentation cameras, or neighbourhood adjacency information). A PettingZoo ParallelEnv wrapper enables drop-in multi-agent reinforcement learning, while a suite of seven task environments, hover, velocity tracking, multi-drone hover, waypoint navigation, formation flight, gate racing, and a generic multi-agent template, demonstrates the breadth of the interface. We describe the environment design, the underlying physics and quadcopter dynamics, and illustrate its use through control and learning examples that mirror those of the closely related gym-pybullet-drones project, while taking advantage of MuJoCo's improved contact handling, rendering, and parallelizability.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Multi-drone simulator for control and RL, enabling safe algorithm development
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多无人机模拟器，支持控制和RL，有助于安全算法开发
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多无人机模拟器，支持控制和RL，有助于安全算法开发
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多无人机模拟器，支持控制和RL，有助于安全算法开发
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a GPU-accelerated multi-drone simulator (MuJoCo-Drones-Gym) supporting arbitrary number of nano-quadcopters for control and RL, enabling high-throughput training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有四旋翼模拟器在物理保真度、多智能体支持和吞吐量之间的权衡，提出了基于MuJoCo物理引擎的GPU加速多无人机模拟器MuJoCo-Drones-Gym。它支持任意数量的Crazyflie 2.x无人机，提供模块化API，可用于控制和强化学习策略的高效开发，有助于无人机安全控制算法的研究。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有四旋翼模拟器在物理保真度、多智能体支持和吞吐量之间的权衡，提出了基于MuJoCo物理引擎的GPU加速多无人机模拟器MuJoCo-Drones-Gym。它支持任意数量的Crazyflie 2.x无人机，提供模块化API，可用于控制和强化学习策略的高效开发，有助于无人机安全控制算法的研究。

quick_skim.title: MARCH: Model-Assisted Reinforcement Learning for the Perceptive Control of Humanoids over Sparse Footholds
quick_skim.abstract: Perceptive bipedal locomotion over sparse terrain remains a difficult challenge: model-based methods are precise but brittle to uncertainty, while model-free methods are robust but struggle to discover the precise, constrained motions required for safety-critical locomotion where small errors can cause catastrophic failures. We propose a model-assisted reinforcement learning (RL) framework that combines both perspectives in three steps: (1) generate a safe reference trajectory using simplified models; (2) train a privileged teacher policy guided by a control Lyapunov function (CLF) reward built around the safe reference trajectory; and (3) distill the teacher into a vision-based student policy. We show that this model-assistance procedure produces physically grounded locomotion, improving sample efficiency, reducing the need for a complex learning curriculum, and achieving smoother locomotion behavior alongside stepping stone performance comparable to model-free baselines. We validate our approach in simulation and demonstrate successful deployment on a Unitree G1 humanoid robot navigating sparse footholds with lateral constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: model-assisted RL with control Lyapunov function reward for safe locomotion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 结合控制李雅普诺夫函数奖励的模型辅助强化学习实现安全运动
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 结合控制李雅普诺夫函数奖励的模型辅助强化学习实现安全运动
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 结合控制李雅普诺夫函数奖励的模型辅助强化学习实现安全运动
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MARCH combines model-based reference trajectory generation with CLF-guided RL reward to achieve safe and robust bipedal locomotion over sparse footholds.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对稀疏立足点上的双足运动，模型辅助强化学习框架MARCH通过三步实现安全控制：先用简化模型生成安全参考轨迹，再以控制李雅普诺夫函数（CLF）作为奖励引导教师策略训练，最后蒸馏为视觉学生策略。CLF奖励确保了约束满足，在仿真和实物上验证了鲁棒性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对稀疏立足点上的双足运动，模型辅助强化学习框架MARCH通过三步实现安全控制：先用简化模型生成安全参考轨迹，再以控制李雅普诺夫函数（CLF）作为奖励引导教师策略训练，最后蒸馏为视觉学生策略。CLF奖励确保了约束满足，在仿真和实物上验证了鲁棒性。

quick_skim.title: Hybrid Neural Network and Conventional Controller Approach for Robust Control of Highly Unstable Systems: Application to Tilt-Rotor Control
quick_skim.abstract: Multirotors are widely used in applications ranging from surveillance to precision agriculture, yet conventional designs remain limited by their under-actuation. Tilt-rotor configurations overcome this limitation by enabling full actuation. This paper investigates neural-network-based control strategies for a fully actuated tilt-rotor system with four thrust-vectoring inputs. Our work is structured in two parts. First, we deliberately present a negative result by evaluating a direct input-output control approach. In this method, multilayer perceptrons (MLPs), long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, and transformer models are trained to map system states and their desired values directly to control signals. We show that this strategy fails to stabilize the system, highlighting the inherent difficulty of applying direct input-output learning to highly unstable plants. Second, as the main contribution, we propose a neural-network-enhanced sliding mode controller (SMC). The method decomposes the system dynamics into input-independent and input-dependent components, with the former learned from a small dataset using lightweight networks, thereby reducing real-time computational demands. Moreover, the proposed method can be trained using flight logs collected from low-performance controllers, and the resulting dynamic model learned from real-world data can be used in simulation. We further compare MLP- and LSTM-based implementations under model uncertainties and external disturbances, demonstrating the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed approach; in particular, the controller with the LSTM plant dynamics predictor achieves superior performance to its MLP-based counterpart while also exhibiting lower runtime.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Neural network control for tilt-rotor UAV
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 倾斜旋翼无人机神经网络控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 倾斜旋翼无人机神经网络控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 倾斜旋翼无人机神经网络控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates neural-network-based control for a fully actuated tilt-rotor UAV, showing direct input-output methods fail while hybrid NN-conventional controller achieves robust control.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文探讨全驱动倾斜旋翼无人机的神经网络控制策略。首先测试MLP、LSTM和Transformer的直接输入输出控制，发现均无法稳定系统；进而提出神经网络与传统控制器混合的方法，利用神经网络补偿模型不确定性，传统控制器保证基础稳定。仿真实验表明混合方法能实现鲁棒控制且性能优于纯传统控制器。为无人机神经网络控制提供了实用设计指南，强调直接端到端学习在高度不稳定系统中的局限性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文探讨全驱动倾斜旋翼无人机的神经网络控制策略。首先测试MLP、LSTM和Transformer的直接输入输出控制，发现均无法稳定系统；进而提出神经网络与传统控制器混合的方法，利用神经网络补偿模型不确定性，传统控制器保证基础稳定。仿真实验表明混合方法能实现鲁棒控制且性能优于纯传统控制器。为无人机神经网络控制提供了实用设计指南，强调直接端到端学习在高度不稳定系统中的局限性。

quick_skim.title: SHAPO: Sharpness-Aware Policy Optimization for Safe Exploration
quick_skim.abstract: Safe exploration is a prerequisite for deploying reinforcement learning (RL) agents in safety-critical domains. In this paper, we approach safe exploration through the lens of epistemic uncertainty, where the actor's sensitivity to parameter perturbations serves as a practical proxy for regions of high uncertainty. We propose Sharpness-Aware Policy Optimization (SHAPO), a sharpness-aware policy update rule that evaluates gradients at perturbed parameters, making policy updates pessimistic with respect to the actor's epistemic uncertainty. Analytically we show that this adjustment implicitly reweighs policy gradients, amplifying the influence of rare unsafe actions while tempering contributions from already safe ones, thereby biasing learning toward conservative behavior in under-explored regions. Across several continuous-control tasks, our method consistently improves both safety and task performance over existing baselines, significantly expanding their Pareto frontiers.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: safe exploration RL method neural network policy
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于神经网络的安全探索强化学习方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于神经网络的安全探索强化学习方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于神经网络的安全探索强化学习方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SHAPO proposes a sharpness-aware policy update rule that implicitly reweights policy gradients to bias learning toward conservatism for safe exploration in RL.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在安全关键领域部署强化学习需要安全探索机制。本文提出SHAPO，一种锐度感知策略优化方法，通过评估扰动参数处的梯度，使策略更新对认知不确定性保持悲观。该方法隐式重新加权策略梯度，放大罕见不安全动作的影响，缓和已安全动作的贡献，从而引导学习向保守方向偏置。理论分析和实验表明SHAPO能有效提升安全探索性能，为安全强化学习提供新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在安全关键领域部署强化学习需要安全探索机制。本文提出SHAPO，一种锐度感知策略优化方法，通过评估扰动参数处的梯度，使策略更新对认知不确定性保持悲观。该方法隐式重新加权策略梯度，放大罕见不安全动作的影响，缓和已安全动作的贡献，从而引导学习向保守方向偏置。理论分析和实验表明SHAPO能有效提升安全探索性能，为安全强化学习提供新思路。
